  • Changelog

Get a formatted and presentable output table for the plausibility checkers

Source: R/pretty_tables.R
pretty_table.Rd

Useful to getting the output returned from the plausibility checkers into a presentable format. It converts scientific notations to standard notations, round values and rename columns to meaningful names.

Usage

generate_pretty_table_mfaz(df)

generate_pretty_table_wfhz(df)

generate_pretty_table_muac(df)

Arguments

df

A summary table object of class data.frame returned by the plausibility checkers.

Value

A data.frame as df. Columns are renamed, values formatted and ready to be shared.

Examples


## Check the plausibility of WFHZ data ----

anthro.01 |>
process_wfhz_data(
sex = sex,
weight = weight,
height = height,
.recode_sex = TRUE
) |>
check_plausibility_wfhz(
sex = sex,
age = age,
weight = weight,
height = height,
flags = flag_wfhz,
area = area
) |>
generate_pretty_table_wfhz()
#> ================================================================================
#> # A tibble: 2 × 20
#>   Area       `Total children` `Flagged data (%)` `Class. of flagged data`
#>   <chr>                 <int> <chr>              <fct>                   
#> 1 District E              505 0.8%               Excellent               
#> 2 District G              686 1.2%               Excellent               
#> # ℹ 16 more variables: `Sex ratio (p)` <chr>, `Class. of sex ratio` <chr>,
#> #   `Age ratio (p)` <chr>, `Class. of age ratio` <chr>, `DPS weight (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. DPS weight` <chr>, `DPS height (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. DPS height` <chr>, `Standard Dev* (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of standard dev` <chr>, `Skewness* (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of skewness` <fct>, `Kurtosis* (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of kurtosis` <fct>, `Overall score` <dbl>, …

## Check the plausibility of MUAC data ----

anthro.01 |>
process_muac_data(
sex = sex,
muac = muac,
age = NULL,
.recode_sex = TRUE,
.recode_muac = FALSE,
unit = "none"
) |>
check_plausibility_muac(
flags = flag_muac,
sex = sex,
muac = muac
) |>
generate_pretty_table_muac()
#> # A tibble: 1 × 9
#>   `Total children` `Flagged data (%)` `Class. of flagged data` `Sex ratio (p)`
#>              <int> <chr>              <fct>                    <chr>          
#> 1             1191 0.3%               Excellent                0.297          
#> # ℹ 5 more variables: `Class. of sex ratio` <chr>, `DPS(#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of DPS` <chr>, `Standard Dev* (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of standard dev` <fct>

## Check the plausibility of MFAZ data ----

anthro.01 |>
process_age(
svdate = "dos",
birdate = "dob",
age = age
) |>
process_muac_data(
sex = sex,
age = "age",
muac = muac,
.recode_sex = TRUE,
.recode_muac = TRUE,
unit = "cm"
) |>
check_plausibility_mfaz(
flags = flag_mfaz,
sex = sex,
muac = muac,
age = age,
area = area
) |>
generate_pretty_table_mfaz()
#> ================================================================================
#> # A tibble: 2 × 18
#>   Area       `Total children` `Flagged data (%)` `Class. of flagged data`
#>   <chr>                 <int> <chr>              <fct>                   
#> 1 District E              505 0.0%               Excellent               
#> 2 District G              686 0.9%               Excellent               
#> # ℹ 14 more variables: `Sex ratio (p)` <chr>, `Class. of sex ratio` <chr>,
#> #   `Age ratio (p)` <chr>, `Class. of age ratio` <chr>, `DPS (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of DPS` <chr>, `Standard Dev* (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of standard dev` <chr>, `Skewness* (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of skewness` <fct>, `Kurtosis* (#)` <dbl>,
#> #   `Class. of kurtosis` <fct>, `Overall score` <dbl>, `Overall quality` <fct>
